options.name: --out
options.description:
Set the name or path of the output <file>. It
defaults to the input filename with its
extension changed to `.txt`, or to match the
generated code when preprocessing, and may be
overridden by template settings. Use `-` instead
of a filename to write to stdout.

options.name: -r
options.description:
Add an <assembly> reference by path or assembly
name. It will be resolved from the framework and
assembly directories.

options.name: --using
options.description:
Import a <namespace> by generating a using
statement.

options.name: -I
options.description:
Add a <directory> to be searched when resolving
included files.

options.name: -P
options.description:
Add a <directory> to be searched when resolving
assemblies.

options.name: --class
options.description:
Preprocess the template into class <name> for use
as a runtime template. The class name may
include a namespace.

options.name: --useRelativeLinePragmas
options.description: Use relative paths in line pragmas.

options.name: --parameter
options.description:
Set session parameter <name> to <value>. The value
is accessed from the template's Session
dictionary, or from a property declared with a
parameter directive: <#@ parameter name='<name>'
type='<type>' #>. If the <name> matches a
parameter directive, the <value> will be
converted to that parameter's type.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Generate debug symbols and keep temporary files.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Output additional diagnostic information to stdout.

options.name: --dp
options.description:
Set <directive> to be handled by directive
processor <class> in <assembly>.

options.name: -a
options.description:
Set host parameter <name> to <value>. It may
optionally be scoped to a <directive> and/or <
processor>. The value is accessed from the host'
s ResolveParameterValue() method or from a
property declared with a parameter directive: <#@
parameter name='<name>' #>.
